volumeTitle: Birds. Lysistrata. Women at the Thesmophoria
label: Birds (Section 1032 - Section 1165)

sections.label: Section 1032
sections.original: οὐκ ἀποσοβήσεις ; οὐκ ἀποίσεις τὼ κάδω ;
sections.translation: Are you not going to get out with your urns ? It ' s not to be believed ; they send us inspectors before we have so much as paid sacrifice to the gods .

sections.label: Section 1035
sections.original: ἐὰν δʼ ὁ Νεφελοκοκκυγιεὺς τὸν Ἀθηναῖον ἀδικῇ —
sections.translation: If the Nephelococcygian does wrong to the Athenian ...

sections.label: Section 1036
sections.original: τουτὶ τί ἔστιν αὖ κακὸν τὸ βιβλίον ;
sections.translation: What trouble now ? What book is that ?

sections.label: Section 1037
sections.original: ψηφισματοπώλης εἰμὶ καὶ νόμους νέους
sections.translation: I am a dealer in decrees , and I have come here to sell you the new laws .

sections.label: Section 1038b
sections.original: τὸ τί ;
sections.translation: Which ?

sections.label: Section 1040
sections.original: χρῆσθαι Νεφελοκοκκυγιᾶς τοῖσδε τοῖς μέτροισι καὶ
sections.translation: The Nephelococcygians shall adopt the same weights , measures and decrees as the Olophyxians .

sections.label: Section 1042
sections.original: σὺ δέ γʼ οἷσπερ ὡτοτύξιοι χρήσει τάχα .
sections.translation: And you shall soon be imitating the Ototyxians .

sections.label: Section 1043
sections.original: οὗτος τί πάσχεις ;
sections.translation: Ow ! what are you doing ?

sections.label: Section 1044
sections.original: οὐκ ἀποίσεις τοὺς νόμους ;
sections.translation: Now will you get out of here with your decrees ? For I am going to let you see some severe ones .

sections.label: Section 1046
sections.original: καλοῦμαι Πισθέταιρον ὕβρεως ἐς τὸν Μουνιχιῶνα μῆνα .
sections.translation: I summon Pisthetaerus for outrage for the month of Munychion .

sections.label: Section 1047
sections.original: ἄληθες οὗτος ; ἔτι γὰρ ἐνταῦθʼ ἦσθα σύ ;
sections.translation: Ha ! my friend ! are you still here ?

sections.label: Section 1050
sections.original: ἐὰν δέ τις ἐξελαύνῃ τοὺς ἄρχοντας καὶ μὴ δέχηται
sections.translation: Should anyone drive away the magistrates and not receive them , according to the decree duly posted ...

sections.label: Section 1052
sections.original: οἴμοι κακοδαίμων , καὶ σὺ γὰρ ἐνταῦθʼ ἦσθʼ ἔτι ;
sections.translation: What ! rascal ! you are back too ?

sections.label: Section 1052a
sections.original: ἀπολῶ σε καὶ γράφω σε μυρίας δραχμάς .
sections.translation: Woe to you ! I ' ll have you condemned to a fine of ten thousand drachmae .

sections.label: Section 1053
sections.original: ἐγὼ δὲ σοῦ γε τὼ κάδω διασκεδῶ .
sections.translation: And I ' ll smash your urns .

sections.label: Section 1054
sections.original: μέμνησʼ ὅτε τῆς στήλης κατετίλας ἑσπέρας ;
sections.translation: Do you recall that evening when you crapped on the column where the decrees are posted ?

sections.label: Section 1055
sections.original: αἰβοῖ · λαβέτω τις αὐτόν . οὗτος οὐ μενεῖς ;
sections.translation: Here ! here ! let him be seized . The Inspector runs off . Why , don ' t you want to stay any longer ? But let us get indoors as quick as possible ; we will sacrifice the goat inside .

sections.label: Section 1.1058
sections.original: ἤδη ʼμοὶ τῷ παντόπτᾳ
sections.translation: Henceforth it is to me that mortals must address

sections.label: Section 1.1060
sections.original: θύσουσʼ εὐκταίαις εὐχαῖς .
sections.translation: their sacrifices and their prayers . Nothing escapes my sight nor my might . My glance embraces the universe , I preserve the fruit in the flower by destroying the thousand kinds of voracious insects the soil produces , which attack the trees

sections.label: Section 1.1065
sections.original: ἐκ κάλυκος αὐξανόμενον γένυσι παμφάγοις
sections.translation: and feed on the germ when it has scarcely formed in the calyx ; I destroy those who ravage the balmy terrace gardens like a deadly plague ;

sections.label: Section 1.1070
sections.original: ἔστιν ὑπʼ ἐμᾶς πτέρυγος ἐν φοναῖς ὄλλυται .
sections.translation: all these gnawing crawling creatures perish beneath the lash of my wing .

sections.label: Section 1072
sections.original: τῇδε μέντοι θἠμέρᾳ μάλιστʼ ἐπαναγορεύεται ,
sections.translation: I hear it proclaimed : A talent for him who shall kill Diagoras of Melos ,

sections.label: Section 1075
sections.original: τῶν τεθνηκότων ἀποκτείνῃ , τάλαντον λαμβάνειν .
sections.translation: and a talent for him who destroys one of the dead tyrants . We likewise wish to make our proclamation : A talent to him among you who shall kill Philocrates , the Struthian ; four , if he brings him to us alive . For this Philocrates skewers the finches together and sells them at the rate of an obolus for seven .

sections.label: Section 1080
sections.original: εἶτα φυσῶν τὰς κίχλας δείκνυσι καὶ λυμαίνεται ,
sections.translation: He tortures the thrushes by blowing them out , so that they may look bigger , sticks their own feathers into the nostrils of blackbirds , and collects pigeons , which he shuts up and forces them , fastened in a net , to decoy others . That is what we wish to proclaim . And if anyone is keeping birds

sections.label: Section 1085
sections.original: εἱργμένους ὑμῶν ἐν αὐλῇ , φράζομεν μεθιέναι .
sections.translation: shut up in his yard , let him hasten to let them loose ; those who disobey shall be seized by the birds and we shall put them in chains , so that in their turn they may decoy other men .

sections.label: Section 1.1088
sections.original: εὔδαιμον φῦλον πτηνῶν
sections.translation: Happy indeed is the race of winged birds who

sections.label: Section 1.1090
sections.original: χλαίνας οὐκ ἀμπισχνοῦνται ·
sections.translation: need no cloak in winter ! Neither do I fear the relentless rays of the fiery dog-days ;

sections.label: Section 1.1095
sections.original: ἡνίκʼ ἂν ὁ θεσπέσιος ὀξὺ μέλος ἀχέτας
sections.translation: when the divine grasshopper , intoxicated with the sunlight , as noon is burning the ground , is breaking out into shrill melody ; my home is beneath the foliage in the flowery meadows . I winter in deep caverns , where I frolic with the mountain nymphs , while in spring I despoil

sections.label: Section 1.1100
sections.original: λευκότροφα μύρτα Χαρίτων τε κηπεύματα .
sections.translation: the gardens of the Graces and gather the white , virgin berry on the myrtle bushes .

sections.label: Section 1102
sections.original: τοῖς κριταῖς εἰπεῖν τι βουλόμεσθα τῆς νίκης πέρι ,
sections.translation: I want now to speak to the judges about the prize they are going to award ; if they are favourable to us , we will load them with benefits far greater than those Paris received .

sections.label: Section 1105
sections.original: πρῶτα μὲν γὰρ οὗ μάλιστα πᾶς κριτὴς ἐφίεται ,
sections.translation: Firstly , the owls of Laurium , which every judge desires above all things , shall never be wanting to you ; you shall see them homing with you , building their nests in your money-bags and laying coins . Besides , you shall be housed like the gods ,

sections.label: Section 1110
sections.original: τὰς γὰρ ὑμῶν οἰκίας ἐρέψομεν πρὸς αἰετόν ·
sections.translation: for we shall erect gables over your dwellings ; if you hold some public post and want to do a little pilfering , we will give you the sharp claws of a hawk . Are you dining in town , we will provide you with stomachs as capacious as a bird ' s crop . But , if your award is against us , don ' t fail to have metal covers fashioned for yourselves ,

sections.label: Section 1115
sections.original: ὥσπερ ἀνδριάντες · ὡς ὑμῶν ὃς ἂν μὴ μῆνʼ ἔχῃ ,
sections.translation: like those they place over statues ; else , look out ! for the day you wear a white tunic all the birds will soil it with their droppings .

sections.label: Section 1118
sections.original: τὰ μὲν ἱέρʼ ἡμῖν ἐστιν ὦρνιθες καλά ·
sections.translation: Birds ! the sacrifice is propitious . But I see

sections.label: Section 1120
sections.original: οὐδείς , ὅτου πευσόμεθα τἀκεῖ πράγματα .
sections.translation: no messenger coming from the wall to tell us what is happening . Ah ! here comes one running himself out of breath as though he were in the Olympic stadium .

sections.label: Section 1122
sections.original: ποῦ ποῦ ʼστι , ποῦ ποῦ ποῦ ʼστι , ποῦ ποῦ ποῦ ʼστι ποῦ ,
sections.translation: Where , where , where is he ? Where , where , where is he ? Where , where , where is he ? Where is Pisthetaerus , our leader ?

sections.label: Section 1123b
sections.original: οὑτοσί .
sections.translation: Here am I .

sections.label: Section 1124
sections.original: ἐξῳκοδόμηταί σοι τὸ τεῖχος .
sections.translation: The wall is finished .

sections.label: Section 1124b
sections.original: εὖ λέγεις .
sections.translation: That ' s good news .

sections.label: Section 1125
sections.original: κάλλιστον ἔργον καὶ μεγαλοπρεπέστατον ·
sections.translation: It ' s a most beautiful , a most magnificent work of art . The wall is so broad that Proxenides , the Braggartian , and Theogenes could pass each other in their chariots , even if they were drawn by steeds as big as the Trojan horse .

sections.label: Section 1129b
sections.original: Ἡράκλεις .
sections.translation: That ' s fine !

sections.label: Section 1130
sections.original: τὸ δὲ μῆκός ἐστι , καὶ γὰρ ἐμέτρησʼ αὔτʼ ἐγώ ,
sections.translation: Its length is one hundred stadia ; I measured it myself .

sections.label: Section 1131b
sections.original: ὦ Πόσειδον τοῦ μάκρους .
sections.translation: A decent length , by Poseidon ! And who built such a wall ?

sections.label: Section 1133
sections.original: ὄρνιθες , οὐδεὶς ἄλλος , οὐκ Αἰγύπτιος
sections.translation: Birds — birds only ; they had neither Egyptian brickmaker , nor stonemason , nor carpenter ;

sections.label: Section 1135
sections.original: ἀλλʼ αὐτόχειρες , ὥστε θαυμάζειν ἐμέ .
sections.translation: the birds did it all themselves ; I could hardly believe my eyes . Thirty thousand cranes came from Libya with a supply of stones , intended for the foundations . The water-rails chiselled them with their beaks . Ten thousand storks were busy making bricks ;

sections.label: Section 1140
sections.original: ὕδωρ δʼ ἐφόρουν κάτωθεν ἐς τὸν ἀέρα
sections.translation: plovers and other water fowl carried water into the air .

sections.label: Section 1142
sections.original: ἐπηλοφόρουν δʼ αὐτοῖσι τίνες ;
sections.translation: And who carried the mortar ?

sections.label: Section 1142b
sections.original: ἐρωδιοὶ
sections.translation: Herons , in hods .

sections.label: Section 1143b
sections.original: τὸν δὲ πηλὸν ἐνεβάλλοντο πῶς ;
sections.translation: But how could they put the mortar into the hods ?

sections.label: Section 1144
sections.original: τοῦτʼ ὦγάθʼ ἐξηύρητο καὶ σοφώτατα ·
sections.translation: Oh ! it was a truly clever invention ;

sections.label: Section 1145
sections.original: οἱ χῆνες ὑποτύπτοντες ὥσπερ ταῖς ἄμαις
sections.translation: the geese used their feet like spades ; they buried them in the pile of mortar and then emptied them into the hods .

sections.label: Section 1147
sections.original: τί δῆτα πόδες ἂν οὐκ ἂν ἐργασαίατο ;
sections.translation: Ah ! to what use cannot feet be put ?

sections.label: Section 1148
sections.original: καὶ νὴ Δίʼ αἱ νῆτταί γε περιεζωσμέναι
sections.translation: You should have seen how eagerly the ducks carried bricks . To complete the tale ,

sections.label: Section 1150
sections.original: ἐπέτοντʼ ἔχουσαι κατόπιν † ὥσπερ παιδία
sections.translation: the swallows came flying to the work , their beaks full of mortar and their trowels on their backs , just the way little children are carried .

sections.label: Section 1152
sections.original: τί δῆτα μισθωτοὺς ἂν ἔτι μισθοῖτό τις ;
sections.translation: Who would want paid servants after this ? But tell me , who did the woodwork ?

sections.label: Section 1154b
sections.original: ὄρνιθες ἦσαν τέκτονες
sections.translation: Birds again ,

sections.label: Section 1155
sections.original: σοφώτατοι πελεκᾶντες , οἳ τοῖς ῥύγχεσιν
sections.translation: and clever carpenters too , the pelicans , for they squared up the gates with their beaks in such a fashion that one would have thought they were using axes ; the noise was just like a dockyard . Now the whole wall is tight everywhere , securely bolted and well guarded ;

sections.label: Section 1160
sections.original: ἐφοδεύεται , κωδωνοφορεῖται , πανταχῇ ,
sections.translation: it is patrolled , bell in hand ; the sentinels stand everywhere and beacons burn on the towers . But I must run off to clean myself ; the rest is your business .

sections.label: Section 1164
sections.original: οὗτος τί ποιεῖς ; ἆρα θαυμάζεις ὅτι
sections.translation: Well ! what do you say to it ? Are you not astonished

sections.label: Section 1165
sections.original: οὕτω τὸ τεῖχος ἐκτετείχισται ταχύ ;
sections.translation: at the wall being completed so quickly ?
